priority_rows.title: Secure sandbox execution plane
priority_rows.problem: Multi-agent task/file execution needs isolated runtime boundaries and explicit capability control.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement REQ-15-001 sandbox contract with bounded filesystem/workdirs, policy gating, and deterministic receipts.

priority_rows.title: Ontological integrity operation gate lane
priority_rows.problem: Flux operations must fail closed when receipt chain or covenant validity is broken.

priority_rows.title: Layered kernel shedding feature topology (layer0 -> layer3)

priority_rows.title: Covenant/drift fail-closed adaptation gates
priority_rows.problem: Real-time adaptation requires strict safety bounds to prevent governance drift during incremental updates.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement REQ-19-004 fail-closed covenant and drift gates for adaptation updates with reversible blocks and receipts.

priority_rows.title: Security-layer inventory with evidence links
priority_rows.problem: Security-layer claims must be fully traceable to enforceable code/policy and test evidence.

priority_rows.title: Per-shadow budget governance layer
priority_rows.problem: Global budgets exist, but fairness and isolation require shadow-level token/compute quota enforcement.

priority_rows.title: Coherence preference runtime verifier lane
priority_rows.problem: Probabilistic pathing requires measurable coherence scoring and deterministic fallback guarantees.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement REQ-23-003 coherence-scored selection telemetry with replay-verified fallback behavior.

priority_rows.title: Strict schema enforcement for persona/lens outputs

priority_rows.title: Conduit edge provider adapter (edge_inference/edge_status)

priority_rows.title: Sandboxed tool execution for external actions

priority_rows.title: Interaction-triggered real-time adaptation loop

priority_rows.title: Native browser daemon binary path

priority_rows.title: Hardware-aware local routing hardening

priority_rows.title: Dynamic tool-context management plane
priority_rows.problem: External tool reliability and precision improve with explicit context routing and deterministic tool selection.

priority_rows.title: Evidence-first audit dashboard drilldown

priority_rows.title: On-device low-power adaptation runtime profile

priority_rows.title: Flux conformance matrix and traceability lane

priority_rows.title: Comparative browser benchmark publication lane

priority_rows.title: Shadow/persona bridge for tools and notifications

priority_rows.title: Persistent Fractal Engine Meta-Organ (Loop-5 Autopilot)
priority_rows.problem: Trigger bridge exists, but a persistent self-evolution organ that continuously mutates habits/memory/routing under strict gates is still missing as a first-class runtime primitive.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/autonomy/persistent_fractal_meta_organ.ts` + policy for nightly/high-success trigger gating, bounded domain mutations, isolated shadow trials, governed live promotion hooks, and deterministic rollback commands (`memory/tools/tests/persistent_fractal_meta_organ.test.js`). Also added TypeScript-first fractal engine substrate under `systems/fractal/` (, , , , , , , , , `README.md`) with hard invariants for tier gating, event-sourced mutation records, soul-anchor refresh, and tier3+ human second-gate enforcement. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-019:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-019; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/fractal`.

priority_rows.title: Vault and Security Shared Rust Core (ZK/FHE Envelope)
priority_rows.problem: Security/vault primitives are not yet fully consolidated under one Rust-first cryptographic runtime with immutable fail-closed behavior.
priority_rows.acceptance: Consolidate vault/security compute primitives into shared Rust core with enforced fail-closed covenant behavior and mobile-safe performance constraints. Verify via tamper simulation + crypto policy conformance receipts (`seal <4ms`, background heap growth `0`) and rollback by restoring prior hardened security profiles under emergency policy gate. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/security_vault.rs`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/econ_crypto.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_004_security_vault_core.json`.

priority_rows.title: Adaptation Channel ABI (Rust Trait + WASM Guest Contract + Generic Fallback)
priority_rows.problem: Cross-system portability requires a governed extension interface rather than scattered per-platform conditionals in core runtime paths.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/ops/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime.{ts,js}` + `config/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime_policy.json` + `config/platform_adaptation_channels.json` to enforce a governed channel ABI/registry with signed attestation metadata, explicit host predicates, deterministic activation receipts, and fail-closed generic fallback when no specific channel matches. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-210:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-210; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Platform Oracle HostProfile Service (Burn-Limiter Expansion)
priority_rows.problem: Steward-agnostic adaptation needs one authoritative, typed host identity contract before any channel or lane can safely activate.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile.{ts,js}` + `config/platform_oracle_hostprofile_policy.json` to run boot/promotion/periodic host probing with signed `HostProfile` receipts (OS/distro/variant/kernel/runtime/cloud/hardware/capabilities), fail-closed minimal fallback, and rollback to last-known-good profile; deterministic artifacts are written to `state/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile/` and covered by `memory/tools/tests/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-209:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-209; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile.ts`.

priority_rows.title: HostProfile Conformance Formal Gate (Lane-Activation Proof Contract)
priority_rows.problem: Without machine-checkable host compatibility proofs, new lanes can bypass adaptation policy and run with invalid assumptions.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/ops/host_profile_conformance_formal_gate.{ts,js}` + `config/host_profile_conformance_formal_gate_policy.json` + `config/host_profile_lane_predicates.json` with per-lane predicate/proof-stub validation, active-lane host-match enforcement, chaos/adversarial check stubs, and signed proof receipts; proof stubs in `docs/proofs/platform/*` and regression coverage in `memory/tools/tests/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-214:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-214; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/host_profile_conformance_formal_gate.ts`, `docs/proofs/platform/alpine_lane_predicate.md`.

priority_rows.title: Rust execution autoscale/token-usage/queue-normalization/criteria-gate/policy-hold/receipt-verdict primitive migration
priority_rows.problem: Increase real Rust execution-path coverage while preserving behavior in autonomy runtime
priority_rows.acceptance: `crates/execution/src/autoscale.rs` now owns `dynamic_caps`, `token_usage`, `normalize_queue`, `criteria_gate`, `policy_hold`, and `receipt_verdict` primitives; `systems/autonomy/backlog_autoscale_rust_bridge.ts` maps explicit mode payloads (`plan`, `batch_max`, `dynamic_caps`, `token_usage`, `normalize_queue`, `criteria_gate`, `policy_hold`, `receipt_verdict`); `systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts` routes queue-pressure normalization, execution token usage, pre-exec criteria gate checks, route policy-hold decisions, and verify-receipt core verdict through Rust with deterministic TS fallback; regression + sovereignty checks: `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_backlog_autoscale_rust_bridge.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_execution_token_usage_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_criteria_gate_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_policy_hold_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_verify_execution_receipt_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/execution_security_gate_integration.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Legion Geas Protocol (Iron Oath Self-Destruct Covenant)
priority_rows.problem: As Red Legion capability scales, autonomy safety requires automatic, multi-layered containment that guarantees compromised/out-of-scope cells cannot persist.
priority_rows.acceptance: Deliver geas lease manager + validation pipeline + instant self-destruct path, layered containment controls, Phoenix continuity handoff, emergency operator override, and full JetStream/Jigsaw evidence with non-regression tests across spawn/security/runtime lanes. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-031 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-031; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Competitive Observability + Benchmark Pack (Trace/Evals + Framework Parity)
priority_rows.problem: Operational confidence and market credibility require explicit trace/eval UX and repeatable benchmark parity artifacts against common alternatives.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/ops/competitive_observability_benchmark_pack.{ts,js}` + policy `config/competitive_observability_benchmark_policy.json`; user-specific benchmark preferences remain in with adaptive scenario weighting in `adaptive/ops/benchmarks/`; permanent benchmark scenarios, trace/eval collectors, and parity report generators remain in `systems/ops/` + `config/`; outputs deterministic parity scorecards for Letta/Mastra/LangGraph/OpenFang lanes without storing user-private contents in benchmark fixtures. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-174:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-174; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/competitive_observability_benchmark_pack.ts`, `systems/ops`, `config`.

priority_rows.title: Modular System-Wide Settling + Rust BlobLoader
priority_rows.problem: Core-only settling leaves major lanes/organs on mixed runtime paths and misses module-level optimization and isolation benefits.
priority_rows.acceptance: Extend settling to module granularity across major organs/lanes with independent settled artifacts and a Rust `BlobLoader` that discovers, memory-maps, and wires module blobs at runtime; require per-module parity/perf receipts and rollback-safe fallback to source/runtime profile when module settle fails. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-006 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-006; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Security + Vault Rust Core Hardening
priority_rows.problem: Security/vault paths require maximum memory-safety and deterministic failure semantics to preserve fail-closed sovereignty guarantees.
priority_rows.acceptance: Migrate security/vault crypto-critical hot paths to Rust (key operations, rotation checks, audit proof emitters) with strict fail-closed behavior and policy continuity. Verify via security regression pack + tamper simulation receipts; rollback by disabling Rust security lane and restoring previous hardened path.

priority_rows.title: Rust Spine Microkernel (Control-Plane Core Extraction)
priority_rows.problem: Code and regression evidence are present but registry status remained queued, which blocked dependent queue execution.
priority_rows.acceptance: Status reconciled to done based on existing implementation, verification tests, and rollback-ready control surfaces already present in runtime/policy lanes. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_race_034_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Relocatable Path Contract (Absolute-Path Eradication)
priority_rows.problem: Hardcoded host paths reduce portability and make fresh-instance bootstrap brittle.
priority_rows.acceptance: Eliminate absolute `/Users/...`-style paths from runtime/config/tooling surfaces, add workspace-root resolver primitive (`$OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE` fallback to repo root), and enforce via CI/path-contract check with fail-closed policy allowlist for fixtures/tests only. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_race_120_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: FluxLattice Migration & Adoption Bridge (`protheusctl migrate`)
priority_rows.problem: Without an explicit migration/adoption bridge, operators cannot safely move between legacy runtime paths and FluxLattice substrate features.
priority_rows.acceptance: Extend `protheusctl migrate` with FluxLattice-aware migration profiles, dry-run diff output, and rollback checkpoints; publish migration runbook for standalone and in-repo adoption modes. Verify with migration simulation tests, compatibility receipts, and reversible checkpoint drills. Rollback by forcing legacy migration profile and disabling FluxLattice migration adapters.

priority_rows.title: Permanent Guidelines Drift Gate + Ticket Output Contract
priority_rows.problem: Operator prompt/governance drift can silently weaken sovereignty and containment rules even when core code paths remain intact.
priority_rows.acceptance: Publish canonical permanent-guidelines artifact, enforce checksum/drift checks against referenced policy docs, require ticket output contract markers, and fail CI on divergence from covenant-first governance baseline. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-conf-007:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-conf-007; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/permanent_guidelines_drift_gate.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Five-System Adaptation Channel Pack (Ubuntu/FreeBSD/NixOS/RaspberryPiOS/Alpine)
priority_rows.problem: Platform-specific capabilities must stay isolated to matched hosts to avoid drift, policy violations, and unnecessary runtime weight.
priority_rows.acceptance: Delivered the five specific adaptation channels in `config/platform_adaptation_channels.json` (`ubuntu`, `freebsd`, `nixos`, `raspios`, `alpine`) plus `generic`, with strict HostProfile predicates, non-match exclusion, and fallback behavior validated by `platform_adaptation_channel_runtime` and regression-covered in `memory/tools/tests/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-211:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-211; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`.

priority_rows.title: Covenant Enforcement Across Flux/Morph/Shadow Operations
priority_rows.problem: Dynamic runtime operations (flux changes, blob morphing, shadow swaps) are not yet uniformly blocked by Covenant checks at the operation boundary, risking inconsistent sovereignty enforcement.
priority_rows.acceptance: Enforce Mind Sovereignty Covenant checks for every flux node move, blob reshape, and shadow swap; on deny emit deterministic `Covenant denied.` receipt and abort operation, on allow emit `Covenant affirmed.` receipt visible in Reasoning Mirror + `protheus-top`. Verify via operation replay tests and signed receipt-chain checks covering both allow/deny paths. Rollback by feature-flagging operation-level Covenant hooks off and reverting to baseline policy gate path.

priority_rows.title: Execution Deterministic Runtime Rust Cutover + Replay Proofs
priority_rows.problem: Execution state machine and replay paths still rely on mixed-language orchestration that can introduce drift and overhead under high cadence workloads.
priority_rows.acceptance: Complete Rust execution runtime + deterministic replay receipt proofs with WASM verification and mobile-safe bindings, retaining TS CLI parse layer only. Verify via 1000-run drift tests + replay parity receipts (`step p95 <5ms`, battery impact `<1.2%/24h`) and rollback by switching runtime selector to prior execution engine. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/execution_replay.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_002_execution_core.json`, `memory/tools/tests/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Cross-Platform Accelerator HAL (CPU/GPU/NPU Offload Contract)
priority_rows.problem: Model/runtime placement needs one typed hardware abstraction across DirectML, Metal, and Vulkan to avoid platform-specific routing drift.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement typed HAL contract exposing accelerator capabilities and offload hints with provider adapters (Windows/macOS/Linux), enforce fail-closed fallback to CPU baseline on mismatch, and emit verification + rollback receipts for every adapter promotion. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-218:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-218; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_queue_executor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Phoenix Protocol (Immortal Red-Team Respawn + Continuity Inheritance)
priority_rows.problem: Defensive pressure increases system resilience only if core red-team capability cannot be permanently neutralized by one successful kill/tamper action.
priority_rows.acceptance: Deliver red-team down-detection + auto-respawn orchestration with inherited tactical memory, bounded sub-2s recovery target, JetStream receipts for down/revive cycles, fractal post-respawn adaptation, and non-regression coverage for spawn/security/runtime paths.

priority_rows.title: Core Settling Engine (Compile + Memory-Map + Re-exec)
priority_rows.problem: Runtime hot paths still carry avoidable overhead from mixed execution layers and process indirection after initialization.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/settle/settler.rs` and `protheusctl settle` to compile active-core artifacts into a sealed native runtime image, memory-map the image, and re-exec into settled mode with deterministic health checks; verify parity/performance receipts and explicit rollback by reverting to pre-settle runtime profile. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-001 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-001; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Reliability Certification Program (SLO/Error-Budget Gate)
priority_rows.problem: Reliability evidence is distributed across lanes and not promoted as one enforceable release contract.
priority_rows.acceptance: Define tiered SLO catalog and error-budget policy for critical services/CLI paths; add release gate that blocks promotion when burn-rate exceeds policy; emit monthly reliability scorecard receipts (`uptime`, `p95/p99`, `incident_rate`, `change_fail_rate`) with drill evidence and rollback drill receipts.

priority_rows.title: Directive gate runtime collapse to compact Rust-ready wrapper
priority_rows.problem: `systems/security/directive_gate.ts` still held a large TypeScript policy evaluator that duplicated Rust `execution_core directive-gate` behavior and inflated TS surface area.
priority_rows.acceptance: Replaced directive gate module with compact compatibility wrapper: runtime default is now Rust-preferred (`DIRECTIVE_GATE_RUST_MODE=prefer`), with deterministic JS fallback retained for fail-open binary unavailability and exported API/constants (`evaluateTask`, `logGateDecision`, `HIGH_RISK_PATTERNS`, `DENY_PATTERNS`, allowlist/trust helpers) preserved. Regression coverage passes in `memory/tools/tests/directive_gate.test.js`, plus execution parity/sovereignty suites. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.

priority_rows.title: Lockweaver Eternal Flux Field (Origin-Lock Verified Structural Flux)
priority_rows.problem: Static obfuscation eventually plateaus; a controlled self-mutating structural defense field raises reverse-engineering cost persistently while preserving trusted runtime behavior.
priority_rows.acceptance: Deliver `systems/security/lockweaver/` daemon+policy pack with origin-lock verification, mutation traversal/reseed loop, strict exclusions, JetStream receipts, threat-adaptive cadence, and rollback drills validated by non-regression/perf tests.

priority_rows.title: Mega Sprint 1 Public 50 Percent Rust Migration (8 Crates + Blobized Core Paths)
priority_rows.problem: Public repository language composition remains overwhelmingly TypeScript, and prior internal/weighted migration signals do not guarantee externally visible Rust ownership at the required threshold.
priority_rows.acceptance: Execute all eleven mega-sprint steps in strict sequence: migrate `execution`, `pinnacle`, `vault`, `red_legion`, `observability`, `graph`, `swarm`, and `mobile` to crate-first Rust ownership; integrate blob fold/unfold + manifest in all eight crates; maintain thin backward-compatible TS wrappers; update root Cargo workspace; and complete full parity + WASM + battery proof suite. Completion requires deterministic artifact bundle: git diff summary for `crates/` and `systems/`, successful `wasm32-unknown-unknown --release` builds for each migrated crate, and public GitHub language-bar evidence showing Rust `>=50%` with TypeScript decrease. Completion is hard-blocked pending explicit human approval after audit. Commit contract: `feat(rust-migration): Mega Sprint 1 - visible 50% Rust + 8 core crates + blob integration`. BLOCKED evidence: `state/ops/rust50_mega_sprint1_batch/latest.json` (`rust_share_pct_after=6.253`, `rust_share_min_pct=50`, violation `rust_share_min_ok`).

priority_rows.title: Sandbox runtime substrate decision (container vs lightweight jail)
priority_rows.problem: Full sandbox rollout depends on approved runtime substrate across target environments and security policy.
priority_rows.acceptance: Approve sandbox substrate strategy and publish policy contract before full implementation rollout.

priority_rows.title: Legacy dependency anchor (V3-RACE-023)
priority_rows.problem: This prerequisite ID is referenced by active backlog rows but was not present in canonical registry rows after parsing.
priority_rows.acceptance: Canonical anchor added so dependency checks can verify this prerequisite as satisfied; no new execution lane is required and rollback remains the original upstream lane fallback. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_registry.js`.

priority_rows.title: FluxLattice Standalone Crate + CLI Surface
priority_rows.problem: Core settle/flux capabilities are currently embedded in the monorepo runtime, which limits reuse, external adoption, and clean substrate packaging.
priority_rows.acceptance: Create `crates/fluxlattice` with stable APIs for settle/morph/flux ops and ship `fluxlattice` CLI (`init`, `settle`, `morph`, `status`) wired to existing receipt and covenant contracts. Verify via cargo workspace build/tests plus parity checks against equivalent `protheusctl` flows with signed receipts. Rollback by disabling crate integration paths and restoring canonical runtime-only command surface.

priority_rows.title: Rust Microkernel Full Control-Plane Extraction + Default Cutover
priority_rows.problem: Rust kernel slices exist, but the default runtime still retains mixed control-plane execution paths that limit race-scale throughput/safety guarantees.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/ops/rust_control_plane_cutover.ts` + `systems/rust/control_plane_component_shim.ts` + policy for parity harnessing, benchmark/SLO-gated default activation, emergency-only JS fallback routing, and staged JS hot-path deprecation receipts (`memory/tools/tests/rust_control_plane_cutover.test.js`).

priority_rows.title: Compute-Tithe Flywheel (Tithe-as-Leverage GPU Donation System)
priority_rows.problem: Add a governed economic engine where verified donated compute reduces effective tithe while feeding high-value runtime capacity for fractal/autonomy workloads.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implemented `systems/economy/` compute-tithe lane (`tithe_engine`, `gpu_contribution_tracker`, `tithe_ledger`, `contribution_oracle`, `discount_policy`, `smart_contract_bridge`, `public_donation_api`, `flywheel_acceptance_harness`) with bracketed discount tiers, second-gate risk enforcement, constitution checks, control-plane event-stream publication, soul GPU patron markers, guard/fractal/routing/model/risk integration hints, and open-platform compatibility API `platform/api/donate_gpu.*`; acceptance coverage added in `memory/tools/tests/compute_tithe_flywheel.test.js` plus docs in `docs/ECONOMY.md`. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-022:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-022; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/economy`, `platform/api/donate_gpu.ts`.

priority_rows.title: `protheus-edge` Lightweight Runtime Package (Reflex + MemFS Cache + Remote Spine Sync)
priority_rows.problem: Current phone readiness is profile-driven, but there is no dedicated edge package contract for portable mobile runtime deployment.
priority_rows.acceptance: Shipped `packages/protheus-edge/` package API + wrappers and `systems/edge/protheus_edge_runtime.js` policy lane with signed sync hashes, cache snapshots, deterministic offline fallback, verification receipts, and rollback path.

priority_rows.title: PsycheForge Adaptive Counter-Profile Defense Organ
priority_rows.problem: Static deception/containment responses are less effective than adaptive attacker-specific defense selection under repeated probing.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implemented `systems/security/psycheforge/` (`psycheforge_organ`, `profile_synthesizer`, `countermeasure_selector`, `temporal_profile_store`) with typed behavioral classification + drift scoring, encrypted temporal profile storage, Rust hot-state persistence bridge (`set-hot-state`), tier3+ shadow-to-live two-gate promotion semantics, integrated hint outputs for guard/redteam/venom/fractal lanes, and receipted verification in `memory/tools/tests/psycheforge_organ.test.js` plus docs update in `docs/SECURITY.md`.

priority_rows.title: WASI2 Execution Completeness Gate (TS Lane -> WASM Runtime)

priority_rows.acceptance: Status reconciled to done based on existing implementation, verification tests, and rollback-ready control surfaces already present in runtime/policy lanes. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_queue_executor.ts`, `systems/ops/wasi2_execution_completeness_gate.ts`, `docs/WASI2_EXECUTION_COMPLETENESS_GATE.md`.

Regression + parity checks pass (`memory/tools/tests/autonomy_sorted_counts_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_policy_hold_classification.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/protheusctl_dispatch_security_gate.test.js`).

priority_rows.problem: Time-to-value scoring remained TypeScript-only, leaving a shared prioritization primitive outside Rust parity for candidate ranking and value signal composition.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `time_to_value_score` autoscale mode in `crates/execution/src/autoscale.rs`, extended bridge mode routing in `systems/autonomy/backlog_autoscale_rust_bridge.ts`, and switched `timeToValueScore` to Rust-first evaluation with bounded cache + deterministic TS fallback. priority_rows.acceptance: Added `proposal_score` autoscale mode in `crates/execution/src/autoscale.rs`, extended bridge mode routing in `systems/autonomy/backlog_autoscale_rust_bridge.ts`, and switched `proposalScore` to Rust-first evaluation with bounded cache + deterministic TS fallback. priority_rows.title: Entrypoint Runtime Contract Standardization
priority_rows.problem: Mixed wrapper/bootstrap indirection makes runtime behavior harder to reason about and failure modes less explicit.
priority_rows.acceptance: Define one production entrypoint contract (dist JS) and one development contract (`tsx`/typed runner), migrate primary entrypoints, keep `ts_bootstrap` only as explicit compatibility lane with missing-bootstrap detection + parity tests. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_race_116_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Sovereign Token + Global Directive Fund Layer
priority_rows.problem: Existing tithe/blockchain lanes lack a unified internal token contract and explicit user-configurable global-fund allocation path.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/economy/protheus_token_engine.{ts,js}` + `systems/economy/global_directive_fund.{ts,js}` with policy contracts; user-specific allocation/opt-in preferences persist under `memory/economy/preferences/` and adaptive weighting/index under `adaptive/economy/preferences/`; shared tokenomics, governance rules, and settlement logic remain in `systems/economy/` + `config/`; stablecoin bridge hooks reuse `V3-BLK-001` controls. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-130:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-130; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Execution Crate Public-Language-Bar Rust Cutover (No Weighted Tricks)
priority_rows.problem: Prior execution migration evidence can satisfy weighted governance while still under-delivering public repository language-bar impact and keeping too much logic outside `crates/execution/`.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement real `crates/execution/` crate (`cdylib` + `wasm32-unknown-unknown` target) that owns deterministic workflow runner, receipt generation, and state machine logic; retain only a thin TS wrapper in `systems/execution/index.ts` through stable FFI. Verify with full parity tests, successful `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release`, full git diff evidence, and tracked line-count delta showing Rust increase and TypeScript decrease (`git ls-files | xargs wc -l` baseline vs after). Rollback by feature-flagged runtime selector restoring previous execution lane and preserving replay compatibility receipts. Completion is blocked until explicit human approval after proof bundle review. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover.ts`, `config/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover_policy.json`, `memory/tools/tests/rust50_conf001_execution_cutover.test.js`, `state/ops/rust50_conf001/latest.json`.

priority_rows.title: `protheus-core` Runtime Envelope Tightening (`<5MB`, `<200ms`, Optional-Flag Matrix)
priority_rows.problem: Current core extraction is complete, but envelope targets in this intake are stricter than existing baseline contracts.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add strict package-size/cold-start benchmark gate for `packages/protheus-core` with optional-module flag matrix tests; fail CI on envelope drift and emit trend receipts.

priority_rows.title: Runtime Tamper Attestation + Self-Containment
priority_rows.problem: Detect live patching/removal attempts and contain safely
priority_rows.acceptance: SKIN runtime attestation now combines integrity kernel + remote heartbeat posture checks (`integrity_policy_path`, heartbeat latest/quarantine alignment), and can transition to deterministic stasis containment state (`state/security/skin_protection/stasis_state.json`) via `verify/enforce` flows with forensic history receipts. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_skin_002_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Red Legion Chaos + Observability Rust Merge Core
priority_rows.problem: Chaos and observability hot loops remain fragmented, limiting throughput and increasing runtime overhead on always-on phone and edge deployments.
priority_rows.acceptance: Deliver Rust-first chaos + telemetry core with signed proof artifacts, sovereignty scoring, and low-overhead trace emit path for Tauri/mobile bindings. Verify via chaos throughput + telemetry overhead receipts (`telemetry overhead <1ms`, chaos battery `<3%/24h`) and rollback by toggling back to previous chaos/observability adapters. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/red_chaos.rs`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/telemetry_emit.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_005_chaos_observability_core.json`.

priority_rows.title: WASM Component Runtime for Skills/Habits
priority_rows.problem: Language-agnostic secure module execution requires capability-scoped sandboxing and signed components, not only script-level contracts.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/wasm/component_runtime.{ts,js}` + policy `config/wasm_component_runtime_policy.json` with signature verification, capability manifests, and deny-by-default host bindings; user-specific module preferences/overrides live in `memory/wasm/` and adaptive module ranking/cooldowns in `adaptive/wasm/`; permanent runtime/verification logic remains in `systems/wasm/` + `config/`. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-138 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-138; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Hierarchical Memory View Plane (Core/Edit/Summary + Swap-Safe Summarization)
priority_rows.problem: Long-term coherence across model/provider swaps needs deterministic derived memory views, not only raw node stores.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/memory/hierarchical_memory_view_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/hierarchical_memory_view_policy.json`; user-specific view artifacts remain in `memory/views/` with adaptive view-selection heuristics in `adaptive/memory/views/`; permanent derivation/summarization contracts remain in `systems/memory/` + `config/`; model-swap path emits before/after parity receipts and bounded summary drift checks. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-172 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-172; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Scheduler + Queue Worker Rust Migration (High-Contention Paths)
priority_rows.problem: Event-loop pressure and queue contention remain core throughput/latency risk for scale tiers.
priority_rows.acceptance: Migrate scheduler/worker hot paths to Rust with parity harness, backpressure invariants, and benchmark proof (throughput, p95/p99, CPU/memory); verify canary promotion receipts and tested rollback to TS worker path. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`.

priority_rows.title: Pinnacle CRDT Rust Engine Phone-First
priority_rows.problem: Offline merge/conflict resolution remains partially non-Rust, limiting deterministic convergence guarantees on multi-device background sync.
priority_rows.acceptance: Move Pinnacle CRDT merge engine fully to Rust with WASM/Tauri mobile background sync hooks and per-merge sovereignty checks. Verify via convergence replay suite + suspend/resume sync receipts (`merge <2ms`, idle battery `<0.5%/24h`) and rollback by reverting to previous merge adapter lane. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/crdt_merge.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_003_crdt_core.json`, `memory/tools/tests/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Sprint 1 Visible Rust Migration Batch (Execution/Pinnacle/Vault/Red Legion)
priority_rows.problem: Existing migration evidence can pass internal gates while under-delivering visible public repository Rust share changes and complete crate-first ownership across critical modules.
priority_rows.acceptance: Execute Sprint 1 strict sequence across four crate-first migrations (`execution`, `pinnacle`, `vault`, `red_legion`), blob integration in all crates, thin TS wrappers, full parity + WASM + battery receipts, and workspace updates. Completion proof must include full `crates/` + `systems/` diffs, successful WASM builds for each migrated crate, and tracked/public Rust language increase evidence with TS decrease. Commit contract: `feat(rust-migration): Sprint 1 - visible Rust increase + 4 core crates + blob integration`. Completion blocked until explicit human audit approval. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_sprint1_batch.ts`, `config/rust50_sprint1_batch_policy.json`, `state/ops/rust50_sprint1_batch/latest.json`.

priority_rows.acceptance: Add iOS/iPadOS integration adapters for continuity/handoff/control/private-relay-aware networking, require verification receipts for security/UX parity, and include rollback-safe disable path to baseline mobile runtime when integration checks fail. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-250 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-250; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Execution Runtime Rust Cutover (Deterministic Receipts + Replay Core)
priority_rows.problem: Deterministic execution/replay core still carries avoidable runtime overhead and correctness risk under heavy concurrency.
priority_rows.acceptance: Move execution/replay hot paths into Rust with strict receipt determinism and replay parity guarantees while retaining existing CLI/operator surfaces. Verify via replay parity suites + p95/p99 throughput receipts; rollback by routing execution to prior TS runtime path.

priority_rows.title: Memory 100 Percent Rust Core + WASM Mobile Bindings
priority_rows.problem: Memory logic remains split across TS and Rust, limiting deterministic performance, battery control, and share growth toward 50 percent Rust by critical module weight.
priority_rows.acceptance: Migrate memory core hot paths to Rust crate-first architecture with wasm32/iOS/Android targets, preserve thin TS wrapper only, and enforce call-level sovereignty + signed receipts. Verify via device-profile benchmark receipts (`recall <3ms`, memory call p95 `<5ms`, battery impact `<0.8%/24h`) and rollback by feature-flagging Rust memory lane to prior adapter profile. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `systems/hybrid/rust/src/memory_hotpath.rs`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/artifacts/v6_rust50_001_memory_core.json`, `memory/tools/tests/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Sprint-Mode Enforcer + Batch Audit Contract
priority_rows.problem: Repeated micro-prompt execution increases orchestration drift and weakens enforcement traceability for high-stakes migration sprints.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement sprint-mode contract requiring: enforcer preamble acknowledgment, single-batch ordered execution plan, immutable no-skip/no-premature-done rule, and end-of-sprint audit artifact summarizing completion, blockers, and proof references. Verify via sprint contract receipts and policy-gate tests; rollback by reverting to existing per-item lane execution flow with advisory-only sprint hints. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_sprint_contract.ts`, `config/rust50_sprint_contract_policy.json`, `memory/tools/tests/rust50_sprint_contract.test.js`, `state/ops/rust50_sprint_contract/latest.json`.

priority_rows.title: Route execution policy-hold decision primitive Rust migration (`routeExecutionPolicyHold`)
priority_rows.problem: `routeExecutionPolicyHold` still depended on a TS-side decision shim for summary-field normalization before bridge evaluation, leaving decision preprocessing partially outside `crates/execution`.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added dedicated `route_execution_policy_hold` autoscale mode in `crates/execution/src/autoscale.rs`, extended bridge mode mapping in `systems/autonomy/backlog_autoscale_rust_bridge.ts`, and switched `routeExecutionPolicyHold` to call the Rust decision primitive directly from summary fields. Regression + parity checks pass (`memory/tools/tests/autonomy_route_execution_policy_hold_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/autonomy_policy_hold_rust_parity.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/protheusctl_dispatch_security_gate.test.js`).

priority_rows.title: Autonomy shipped-run counter Rust migration (`shippedCount`)

priority_rows.acceptance: Added `shipped_count` autoscale mode in `crates/execution/src/autoscale.rs`, extended bridge mode routing in `systems/autonomy/backlog_autoscale_rust_bridge.ts`, and switched `shippedCount` to Rust-first counting with bounded cache + deterministic TS fallback. priority_rows.acceptance: Add CUDA execution lane with zero-copy memory paths, graph/stream scheduling, and NVLink-aware multi-GPU orchestration, require verification receipts for performance and parity, and include rollback-safe fallback to existing execution backends on regression. priority_rows.title: Contract-Coupled Defense Dependency Mesh
priority_rows.problem: Removing defenses should break authorization paths, not create undefined runtime behavior
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/security/skin_protection_layer.ts` contract mesh lane (`state_kernel/proposal_gating/execution_primitive/memory_traversal`) with deterministic reason codes; actuation now evaluates this gate in `systems/actuation/actuation_executor.ts` before execution and blocks fail-closed when containment is active; merge + contract checks now assert lane presence (`systems/security/merge_guard.ts`, `systems/spine/contract_check.ts`).

priority_rows.title: Dynamic Blob Morphing
priority_rows.problem: Settled runtime blobs are currently treated as static units, limiting live optimization and preventing fluid in-place reconfiguration.
priority_rows.acceptance: Extend modular settling so active blobs can partially dissolve, reshape hot-path structure, and re-coalesce at runtime without restart; integrate autogenesis-triggered morph intents, require signed morph receipts, and enforce deterministic rollback to last stable blob image.

priority_rows.title: Promotion-Coupled Re-Settlement Policy
priority_rows.problem: Lane promotions currently do not automatically refresh settled artifacts, allowing optimization drift from promoted runtime state.
priority_rows.acceptance: Integrate settlement trigger into promotion/autogenesis pipeline with consent-gated policy, bounded cooldowns, and signed receipts per promotion cycle; verify promotion stability/non-regression and rollback by disabling auto-settle coupling via policy. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-004 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-004; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Settled-State Operator Surface in `protheus-top`
priority_rows.problem: Operators lack a first-class view of settled runtime identity, efficiency gains, and edit/revert readiness state.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add `protheus-top` settled-state panel exposing runtime hash, snapshot lineage, memory/latency deltas, and consent-gated edit-core action status; verify UI/receipt linkage and rollback by feature-gating panel without affecting settle runtime. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-005 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-005; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Personas bootstrap vs dynamic memory split + token budget guard
priority_rows.problem: Persona lens invocations could over-inject dynamic memory context, causing token waste/drift and inconsistent runtime cost in single/multi/triggered loop calls.
priority_rows.acceptance: `systems/personas/cli.ts` now separates bootstrap context from dynamic memory context, enforces configurable pre-invocation context budget (default `2000`) with trim/reject modes, logs over-budget violations to `personas/organization/telemetry.jsonl` and persona correspondence, and emits structured `context_budget` metadata. Loop hooks now pass explicit budget flags in `systems/autonomy/inversion_controller.ts` and `adaptive/rsi/rsi_bootstrap.ts`. Regression + sovereignty checks: `memory/tools/tests/personas_context_budget_guard.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/personas_lens_cli.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/execution_security_gate_integration.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Mind Fortress Principle (Mind Sovereignty Covenant Anchor)
priority_rows.problem: Without an explicit top-level covenant and enforcement path, growing capability layers can drift from the core mission of merged-mind sovereignty and safety.
priority_rows.acceptance: Covenant text is canonical in constitution/docs/code headers, compliance fields are enforced in organ contracts, warden/fractal scoring includes sovereignty-first priority, and CI drift gates fail any mutation that weakens covenant guarantees.

priority_rows.title: Confidence Calibration & Probability Contracts
priority_rows.problem: Binary pass/fail confidence loses ranking quality and obscures uncertainty management.
priority_rows.acceptance: Cross-signal hypotheses now carry calibrated probabilities (`probability_contract`), emit reliability bins + Brier artifacts, and enforce strict calibration contracts via `--strict=1` gate behavior when Brier exceeds policy thresholds. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_race_081_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Multi-Hop Objective Chain Mapper
priority_rows.problem: Signal-to-objective linkage is currently shallow, limiting explainability and prioritization quality.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/sensory/multi_hop_objective_chain_mapper.{ts,js}` + `config/multi_hop_objective_chain_policy.json` to fuse cross-signal hypotheses and latent-intent edges into explainable four-hop paths (`eye -> topic -> implication -> objective`) with confidence-weighted ranking receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/objective_chain_mapper/`; regression coverage in `memory/tools/tests/multi_hop_objective_chain_mapper.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Analysis Quality SLO Contract
priority_rows.problem: Analysis quality must be measured directly, not inferred from execution-only outcomes.

priority_rows.title: Canonical Backlog Registry + Generated Views

priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/ops/backlog_registry.ts` (+ runtime ) with `sync/check/status`, canonical registry output (`config/backlog_registry.json`), generated views (`docs/backlog_views/active.md`, ), strict drift receipts, policy contract, and regression test `memory/tools/tests/backlog_registry.test.js`.

priority_rows.problem: User growth increases attack/abuse surface and noisy-neighbor risk; baseline controls are insufficient without explicit scale hardening contracts.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add tiered rate-limit/abuse controls, tenant-isolation invariants, and auth/session hardening with auditability; validate through adversarial load tests and fail-closed policy checks before each scale-stage promotion. priority_rows.problem: Rust migration effort is currently broad but not explicitly ranked by measurable runtime/cost impact, which risks spending effort on low-yield components.
priority_rows.acceptance: Produce a canonical hotspot inventory (CPU/heap/queue-lag/p95/p99), ranked by expected ROI and risk; include ADR receipts for top candidates, explicit no-migrate list for I/O-bound paths, and verify via reproducible benchmark/check receipts with rollback path to baseline prioritization profile. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`.

priority_rows.title: Memory Retrieval/Index Hot Path Rust Cutover
priority_rows.problem: Retrieval/indexing loops remain high-frequency cost centers and can dominate latency under load.
priority_rows.acceptance: Move retrieval/index hot loops to Rust (vector/ANN/search/transform), require correctness parity + latency/cost receipts under production-like load, and include rollback-safe fallback to prior retrieval implementation. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust_enterprise_productivity_program.js`.

priority_rows.title: Live trading proof gate (30-day promotion evidence)
priority_rows.problem: Operational trust still requires sustained live/paper trading proof under real market conditions.
priority_rows.acceptance: 30-day canary evidence meets promotion PnL/risk/SLO thresholds with deterministic receipts.

priority_rows.title: Horizontal scaling proof in production profile
priority_rows.problem: Single-node execution limits enterprise confidence for failover and sustained load handling.
priority_rows.acceptance: Multi-node failover and soak evidence satisfies distributed runtime gates in production profile.

priority_rows.title: Vault legacy policy engine collapse to Rust-core compatibility wrapper
priority_rows.problem: `systems/vault/legacy_vault.ts` still contained a full TypeScript vault-policy evaluator duplicating logic already owned by Rust `security_core`.
priority_rows.acceptance: Replaced legacy TS evaluator with a compatibility wrapper delegating to Rust-backed `systems/vault/index`, preserving exported API shape (`loadLegacyVaultPolicy`, `evaluateVaultPolicyLegacy`, `policyDigest`) and fail-closed fallback semantics. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.

priority_rows.title: Background Persistent Agent Runtime
priority_rows.problem: Adds always-on, trigger-driven autonomous runtime scheduling as a reusable primitive instead of bespoke loop code.
priority_rows.acceptance: Delivered `systems/autonomy/background_persistent_agent_runtime.ts` + policy `config/background_persistent_agent_runtime_policy.json`; queue/trigger/tick primitive with bounded activations, shadow-first apply control, and integration wiring in `systems/continuum/continuum_core.ts` + `systems/primitives/runtime_scheduler.ts`, validated by `memory/tools/tests/background_persistent_agent_runtime.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/runtime_scheduler.test.js`, and `memory/tools/tests/continuum_core.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Mesh Result Quorum + Provenance Aggregation Contract
priority_rows.problem: Distributed shards require verifiable aggregation and fail-closed reconciliation to prevent poisoned/partial outputs from entering runtime decisions.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement aggregation contract enforcing signature checks, provenance-chain validation, quorum acceptance thresholds, deterministic tie-break/retry policies, rejection receipts for invalid/malicious result sets, and rollback to last-good aggregate on quorum failure. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-201 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-201; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Swift/SwiftUI Co-Runtime Layer (Rust Interop Contracts)
priority_rows.problem: Native Apple UX/performance requirements call for a governed Swift layer while keeping Rust core control-plane authority intact.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add Swift/SwiftUI co-runtime interfaces with explicit Rust interop contracts, require verification receipts for parity and boundary safety, and include rollback-safe reversion to existing UI/runtime surfaces on incompatibility. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-253 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-253; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Horizon Worlds Collaboration/Test Sandbox Adapter
priority_rows.problem: Social-scale environment testing requires dedicated integration with Horizon primitives under governed execution and privacy controls.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add Horizon environment adapter for collaboration and digital-twin test scenarios, require verification receipts for policy-compliant execution and scenario integrity, and include rollback-safe environment disable path when moderation/safety checks fail. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V3-RACE-298 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V3-RACE-298; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Requirement Conformance Matrix + Gate (`external prompt -> canonical lane`)
priority_rows.problem: Repeated external directives can trigger duplicate work unless requirements are normalized into one traceable matrix with drift checks.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added deterministic matrix artifact `config/requirement_conformance_matrix.json`, strict gate `systems/ops/requirement_conformance_gate.{ts,js}` + policy `config/requirement_conformance_gate_policy.json`, and fail-closed checks for missing/unmapped requirements/file anchors/test evidence with coverage in `memory/tools/tests/requirement_conformance_gate.test.js`. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-conf-003:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-conf-003; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/requirement_conformance_gate.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Core Migration Bridge + Workspace Port Command
priority_rows.problem: Repo/org migration without first-class tooling risks user friction, state loss, and trust erosion.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/migration/bridge.rs` plus `protheusctl migrate --to <org/repo> [--workspace=<name>]` to move remotes, backup state, transfer config/habits/vault/memory/scientific receipts, and emit signed migration receipts with rollback-safe checkpoints.

priority_rows.title: `protheus-vault` Zero-Knowledge Secrets CLI
priority_rows.problem: Secret-management primitives are present but not exposed as a cohesive standalone CLI with rotate/audit operator workflows.
priority_rows.acceptance: Deliver `protheus-vault` CLI (`seal`, `rotate`, `audit`) with strict non-plaintext policies, signed audit receipts, and policy-gated recovery controls. Verify via secret-handling tests and fail-closed policy checks; rollback by disabling standalone vault CLI and reverting to existing secrets lane entry points.

priority_rows.title: Conduit Phase 2: Constitution + runtime policy gate binding
priority_rows.problem: TS command ingress must be constitution-safe and policy-bound in Rust
priority_rows.acceptance: `RegistryPolicyGate` enforces AGENT-CONSTITUTION + guard registry checks in Rust; deny-by-default capability map and deterministic `policy_receipt_hash` on every command.

priority_rows.title: 50 Percent Rust Critical-Weight Enforcement Gate
priority_rows.problem: Prior Rust plans target 15-25 percent; no hard governance gate currently enforces progression to 50 percent by critical module weight with anti-fake completion evidence.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add enforced migration gate requiring: critical module-weight share `>=50%` Rust, per-lane committed code diff evidence, test/CI receipts, phone battery receipt bundle, and explicit `PAUSED` state when constraints cannot be met. Verify through governance audit receipts and automated anti-fake completion checks; rollback by downgrading enforcement to advisory mode with expiry and remediation plan. Evidence: `systems/ops/rust50_migration_program.ts`, `config/rust50_migration_program_policy.json`, `docs/RUST50_MIGRATION_IMPLEMENTATION.md`, `state/ops/rust50_migration_program/rust50_gate_state.json`, `memory/tools/tests/rust50_migration_program.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Staged Containment Ramp

priority_rows.title: Preferred Advanced Substrate Detection + Graceful Binary Fallback
priority_rows.problem: Settling currently lacks a deterministic substrate preference chain and auditable fallback behavior for emerging compute targets.
priority_rows.acceptance: During settle, probe in priority order: balanced ternary substrate (hardware/emulator) then qubit access providers (stub/mock-capable). If neither is available, emit exactly once per settle (unless explicit verbose mode) the line: "No ternary substrate or qubit access detected. Reverting to binary mode." Record probe attempts, selected substrate, and fallback reason as signed receipts, keep probes pluggable/future-proof, and verify zero measurable performance regression on standard binary path. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SETTLE-011 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SETTLE-011; receipt must be present in state artifacts. priority_rows.acceptance: Daily summary command/report shows receipt pass rate + top failure reasons Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_003_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Policy for tracked vs ignored state streams
priority_rows.problem: Keep audit logs intentional and reduce accidental tracking drift
priority_rows.acceptance: One doc defines tracked state classes and `.gitignore` alignment Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_004_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Outcome calibration by proposal type (post BL-027 companion)
priority_rows.problem: Track pass-rate by proposal type and tune admission thresholds using per-type shipped/no-change outcomes
priority_rows.acceptance: `systems/autonomy/proposal_type_outcome_calibrator.ts` now emits bounded per-type threshold offsets (strategy policy proposal type threshold offsets) plus per-type audit reasoning, writes calibration receipts on offset changes (`state/adaptive/strategy/receipts.jsonl`), and proposal admission/gating consume offsets via shared outcome-fitness policy helpers

priority_rows.title: Release-gate + canary/rollback enforcement expansion
priority_rows.problem: Existing checks exist but canary/rollback discipline must be mandatory for self-change and high-risk lanes
priority_rows.acceptance: `improvement_controller start-validated` now requires policy-root authorization (lease + approval note) before self-change trial start, and medium-risk executable proposals are blocked when rollback path signals are missing (`medium_risk_missing_rollback_path`) Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_002_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Full repo TypeScript conversion (`.js` -> `.ts`)
priority_rows.problem: Improve long-term maintainability and refactor safety once V1 critical-path typing is stable
priority_rows.acceptance: Core runtime is TS-owned end-to-end (`systems/` + `lib/`), dist-runtime `legacy-pairs --strict=1` is green, and the only non-wrapper JS holdout is `lib/ts_bootstrap.js` by design

priority_rows.title: Global capability switchboard (security-locked)
priority_rows.problem: Allow rapid feature/capability deactivation under drift or threat without allowing model-driven disablement of security controls
priority_rows.acceptance: Implemented in `systems/security/capability_switchboard.ts` with signed policy-root gate, dual-control toggles, non-deactivatable security lanes, and immutable audit history (`config/capability_switchboard_policy.json`)

priority_rows.title: Full directive-to-system value currency propagation
priority_rows.problem: Let each objective define its own success currency (revenue, delivery, quality, etc.) while preserving strong money prioritization when applicable
priority_rows.acceptance: Outcome-fitness now feeds currency-policy overlays and calibration receipts (`systems/autonomy/outcome_fitness_loop.ts`, `lib/outcome_fitness.ts`, `lib/strategy_resolver.ts`) so value-currency adaptation closes the loop

priority_rows.title: Per-candidate adversarial simulation lane (sandboxed micro red-team)
priority_rows.problem: Replace heuristic candidate risk pressure with direct adversarial evidence before promotion
priority_rows.acceptance: `systems/workflow/orchestron/adversarial_lane.ts` now runs per-candidate probes (critical/non-critical findings + replay artifacts), `nursery_tester` consumes candidate-level adversarial evidence and blocks by per-candidate thresholds, and orchestron payload/birth telemetry now emit adversarial probe receipts (`adversarial_scored`, `candidate_adversarial`)

priority_rows.title: Legacy dependency anchor (V2-058)
priority_rows.problem: This prerequisite ID is referenced by active backlog rows but was not present in canonical registry rows after parsing.
priority_rows.acceptance: Canonical anchor added so dependency checks can verify this prerequisite as satisfied; no new execution lane is required and rollback remains the original upstream lane fallback. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v2_058_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Research Organ (long-horizon closed-loop research sub-organ)
priority_rows.problem: Add on-demand deep investigation capacity without bloating core runtime
priority_rows.acceptance: `systems/research/research_organ.ts` can spawn bounded research branches (experiment/critique/synthesis loop), scale depth by trit uncertainty + value priority, and distill outputs into archetype proposals with promotion gates. Spec reference: `tmp/grok_fractal_organs_2026-02-25.md`

priority_rows.title: Legacy dependency anchor (V3-DOC-004)
priority_rows.problem: This prerequisite ID is referenced by active backlog rows but was not present in canonical registry rows after parsing.
priority_rows.acceptance: Canonical anchor added so dependency checks can verify this prerequisite as satisfied; no new execution lane is required and rollback remains the original upstream lane fallback. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_doc_004_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Event-Sourced Control Plane + CQRS Materialized Views
priority_rows.problem: Imperative mutable state limits replay/debug/audit quality at scale.
priority_rows.acceptance: Append-only event log is authoritative for control-plane mutations; materialized views derive from event stream; deterministic replay reconstructs equivalent state for audited windows. Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-003:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-003; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js`.

priority_rows.title: Adversarial Goal-Drift Auditor
priority_rows.problem: Primary objective scoring can be gamed over time; independent adversarial auditing reduces hidden optimization drift.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add second-pass adversarial evaluator with explicit go/no-go veto for promotion/apply when Goodhart or covenant misalignment signals exceed bounds. Evidence: `systems/security/adversarial_goal_drift_auditor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Memory Transport Abstraction Unification
priority_rows.problem: Mixed transport semantics increase complexity and observability inconsistency in memory runtime behavior.
priority_rows.acceptance: Added canonical transport primitive `systems/memory/memory_transport.ts` (runtime wrapper `.js`) and migrated `memory_recall` rust query/get lanes to use one daemon/cli/fallback contract with normalized telemetry (`transport_attempts`, fallback reason, transport detail); added regression coverage in `memory/tools/tests/memory_transport.test.js`.

Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-141:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-141; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/archival/content_addressed_archive.ts`, `systems/archival`, `config`.

Verify via npm run -s lane:v3-race-332:run and npm run -s test:lane:v3-race-332; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected. Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_queue_executor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Optional Hidden Lens Mode + Selective Exposure
priority_rows.problem: Lens documentation currently risks either cluttering public source views or withholding useful context entirely, with no first-class control over selective visibility.
priority_rows.acceptance: Implement `lens-mode` profiles (`hidden` default, `minimal`, `full`) with private lens storage (`.private-lenses/`), selective expose/sync commands, and documented build toggles that leave runtime untouched. Verify via source-diff tests (hidden mode emits zero inline lens tags), lens validation checks for private/public stores, and receipt logs for expose/sync operations. Rollback by forcing global hidden mode and disabling lens exposure/sync commands.

priority_rows.title: Observability + SRE Operations Maturity Pack
priority_rows.problem: Million-user operations require full observability, actionable alerts, and practiced incident response beyond basic telemetry.
priority_rows.acceptance: Complete metrics/tracing/logging coverage for critical flows, define page policies and runbooks with drill SLAs, and verify incident response performance in recurring game-days with auditable outcomes. Verify via node systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.js run --id=V4-SCALE-008 --apply=1 --strict=1 and node memory/tools/tests/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.test.js --id=V4-SCALE-008; receipt must be present in state artifacts. Rollback via git revert of completion commit or canonical status reset to queued if regression is detected.

priority_rows.title: Autonomous long-horizon experiment scheduler
priority_rows.problem: High-value hypotheses lack a governed, reversible scheduling lane for consent-gated experiment execution.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add background experiment scheduler that prioritizes hypotheses by VOI + risk, enforces sandbox + consent timeout-default-deny, emits full scientific receipt chains, verifies scheduling/rollback behavior with integration tests, and supports explicit rollback to no-op scheduling mode. Evidence: `systems/science/experiment_scheduler.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Unchanged-State Admission Gate
priority_rows.problem: Repeated `stop_repeat_gate_unchanged_state` holds are consuming attempt budget without producing net-new actionability.
priority_rows.acceptance: Add pre-admission state-delta checks and semantic dedupe so unchanged proposals are parked before attempt accounting, with explicit reason receipts and configurable freshness windows. Verify with 30-day replay and hold-taxonomy receipts; success criteria: reduce unchanged-state holds by >=50% while preserving shipped yield. Rollback by disabling admission delta gate and reverting to baseline queue policy profile.

priority_rows.title: Distributed Runtime Proof (N+1 Failover + Multi-Node Soak)
priority_rows.problem: Scale posture is still mostly single-node proof; enterprise readiness needs deterministic multi-node resilience evidence under failover and sustained load.
priority_rows.acceptance: Ship deterministic multi-node certification harness with active/passive and active/active failover drills, publish `RTO/RPO` receipts, and enforce promotion gate on `p95/p99`, error budget, and data-consistency invariants during node-loss scenarios; include rollback-to-single-node safety profile with verified recovery receipts.

priority_rows.title: Formal Security Review Program (Threat Model + Independent Review Cadence)
priority_rows.problem: Security controls are present, but there is no formalized recurring independent review program with deterministic closure gates.
priority_rows.acceptance: Establish quarterly security-review program with threat-model refresh, independent reviewer checklist, remediation SLA classes, and merge/release blockers for unresolved critical findings; publish signed review receipts and exception approvals with expiry and rollback controls.

priority_rows.title: External temp-data backup pipeline (outside git)
priority_rows.problem: Preserve high-churn runtime/state data without repo noise
priority_rows.acceptance: `systems/ops/state_backup.js` + `config/state_backup_policy.json`, optional daily spine hook (`STATE_BACKUP_ENABLED=1`), retention policy encoded

priority_rows.title: Ops visibility dashboard + SLO alerts for autonomy health
priority_rows.problem: Make drift/stalls visible before they degrade autonomy quality
priority_rows.acceptance: Daily/weekly report includes dark-eye, proposal starvation, loop stall, and drift SLOs with thresholded alerts Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_026_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Batch execution lane for low-urgency LLM work
priority_rows.problem: Capture major token-cost reductions on deferable workloads without hurting latency-critical tasks
priority_rows.acceptance: Add queueable batch adapter path for low-urgency tasks with deterministic SLA/expiry rules, per-task receipts, and measured token-cost delta vs non-batch baseline Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_041_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Route-task Rust authority hardening (fail-closed on Rust route primitive/match/reflex/complexity outages)
priority_rows.problem: `systems/routing/route_task.ts` still silently fell back to TypeScript matching/classification when Rust outputs were unavailable, leaving duplicated deterministic routing logic active during outages.
priority_rows.acceptance: Route-task now treats Rust route primitives, habit matching, reflex matching, and complexity classification as authoritative by default and returns `MANUAL` with explicit `route_error` when Rust outputs are unavailable/invalid; optional TS fallback is explicit via `ROUTE_TASK_TS_FALLBACK=1`. Regression and sovereignty suites stay green. Verified in March 2026 migration proof run.

priority_rows.title: Outcome correlation expansion (task-type x eye-source x model)
priority_rows.acceptance: Router outcome learning now tracks and scores `task_type x source_eye x model` buckets (`by_task_eye_source`) alongside global/capability/task-type lanes, with source-eye context wired end-to-end from autonomy to route execution (`systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`, `systems/routing/route_execute.ts`, `systems/routing/route_task.ts`, `systems/routing/model_router.ts`) and regression coverage in `memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Focus-controller trigger adaptation cadence tuning
priority_rows.acceptance: Focus controller now computes adaptive cadence (`systems/sensory/focus_controller.ts`) from recent focus pressure + realized outcome score, applies effective refresh windows (trigger + lens) with min-interval anti-thrash guards, and emits cadence telemetry in refresh/status payloads. Policy/store defaults were extended in `systems/adaptive/sensory/eyes/focus_trigger_store.ts` and regression coverage added in `memory/tools/tests/focus_controller.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Baseline adaptive-mutation safety kernel
priority_rows.problem: Any topology/self-improvement expansion requires hard guardrails before promotion
priority_rows.acceptance: Enforce mutation budget cap + TTL + quarantine + veto window + rollback receipt for all adaptive topology proposals, and fail closed when safety attestations are missing Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_006_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Mirror Organ (active self-observation and self-critique)
priority_rows.problem: Upgrade read-only observer into a controlled self-improvement loop
priority_rows.acceptance: Implemented via `systems/autonomy/mirror_organ.ts` + wrapper, with proposal-only outputs, confidence scoring, replayable evidence (`replay --proposal-id`), spine stage (`spine_mirror_organ`), continuum self-improvement integration, and suggestion-lane ingestion from `state/autonomy/mirror_organ/suggestions/`. Coverage: `memory/tools/tests/mirror_organ.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/suggestion_lane.test.js`, `memory/tools/tests/continuum_core.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Global molt cycle (scheduled compaction/pruning)
priority_rows.problem: Keep long-lived organism lean by periodically shedding stale topology/habits/archetypes with safety rollback
priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/ops/global_molt_cycle.ts` + policy (`config/global_molt_cycle_policy.json`) to synthesize 30-day molt plans from atrophy/pathway/assimilation signals, enforce veto + human approval gates, and apply only reversible actions with rollback receipts (`memory/tools/tests/global_molt_cycle.test.js`)

priority_rows.title: Independent child-organ runtime stack for recursive workflows
priority_rows.problem: Move from structural child drafts to true autonomous child organs with isolated lifecycle and governance
priority_rows.acceptance: Child spawn/run/rollback/status now emit bounded-resource verification bundles + rollback contracts per organ lifecycle while retaining deterministic rollback receipts (`memory/tools/tests/child_organ_runtime.test.js`). Evidence: `systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_013_anchor.ts`.

priority_rows.title: Consent-gated cross-workflow learning conduit to master model queue
priority_rows.problem: Convert workflow outcomes into long-horizon learning advantage without policy or rights drift
priority_rows.acceptance: Learning conduit now exposes verification + rollback contracts across ingest/promote/status and adds explicit `rollback` command for entry-stage reversibility; coverage updated in `memory/tools/tests/learning_conduit.test.js`. Evidence: `systems/workflow/learning_conduit.js`.

priority_rows.title: Generative Simulation Mode

priority_rows.title: Cross-Objective Interference Guard for Analysis Changes

priority_rows.acceptance: Added `systems/sensory/cross_objective_interference_guard.{ts,js}` + `config/cross_objective_interference_guard_policy.json` to evaluate multi-objective before/after deltas against per-objective interference budgets and fail promotion when any objective exceeds allowed regression, with guard receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/objective_interference_guard/`; regression coverage in `memory/tools/tests/cross_objective_interference_guard.test.js`.

priority_rows.title: Unified Guard Check Registry (Manifest-Driven Gates)

priority_rows.title: Script Surface Reduction Wave II

priority_rows.acceptance: Delivered `systems/ops/script_surface_reduction_wave2.{ts,js}` + policy (`config/script_surface_reduction_wave2_policy.json`), executed apply lane to retire 213 non-critical `:status` aliases (script count `798 -> 585`), preserved critical/referenced surface, and published migration receipts + command-map diff (`state/ops/script_surface_reduction_wave2/*`, `docs/ops/SCRIPT_SURFACE_REDUCTION_WAVE2.md`).

priority_rows.title: Mobile Competitive Benchmark & CI Matrix (Battery/Thermal/72h Autonomy)
